skill.name: backend-migrations
skill.description: Create and review database schema migrations with attention to reversibility, version control, data integrity, and zero-downtime deployment planning.
skill.summary: Guide safe backend database migration work from planning through rollback review.
skill.author: EIS-ITS

security_audit.summary: The path traversal findings are true pattern matches because the skill references a parent-directory file outside its own folder. No malicious intent or prompt injection was found in SKILL.md, but the external file dependency should be reviewed before publication. The weak cryptography findings are false positives from ordinary migration prose, with no evidence of cryptographic code.

medium_findings.title: Parent Directory File Reference
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md directs the assistant to read a file through a ../../../ relative path. This can pull instructions from outside the packaged skill folder and makes the reviewed behavior depend on external repository content.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The ../../../ sequence is directly present at the reported location. The risk is contextual because it references local documentation, but that documentation is outside the scanned skill package.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithm patterns in prose-only migration guidance. No cryptographic API, hashing operation, encryption call, or credential handling is present in the cited lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain natural-language descriptions of database migration work. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic implementation in SKILL.md.

dangerous_patterns.title: Path Traversal Sequence In Documentation Link
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses a ../../../ relative link to reach a file outside the skill directory. This is not confirmed malware, but it weakens review boundaries for marketplace packaging.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The path traversal sequence is explicit and repeated in a single link. The surrounding text frames it as a documentation reference, so severity is medium rather than high.

content.value_statement: Database schema changes can break deployments, block rollbacks, or corrupt production data. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code plan reversible migrations with safer release steps.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation of database migration files for schema changes.
Encourages reversible up and down migration planning.
Supports review of indexes, tables, columns, and database version history.
Prompts separation of schema migrations from data migrations.
Supports troubleshooting failed migrations and inconsistent database states.
limitations: It does not execute database migrations or connect to a database.
It depends on a referenced standards file outside the skill folder.
It must be adapted to the project's migration framework and database engine.
It cannot guarantee production safety without tests, backups, and review.

use_cases.title: Add a New Schema Change
use_cases.description: Plan a migration for a new table, column, or index with rollback behavior and compatibility checks.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Deployment
use_cases.description: Review a migration sequence for zero-downtime release risks before it reaches production.

use_cases.title: Audit Migration Safety
use_cases.description: Check existing migration files for reversibility, data integrity concerns, and risky schema operations.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Migration
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a small schema change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a backend migration plan for adding a nullable column. Include the up step, rollback step, tests, and deployment notes.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Migration File
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of an existing migration.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this migration for reversibility, locking risk, index safety, and backwards compatibility. List required changes before merge.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Zero-Downtime Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced release planning across application and database changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a zero-downtime migration sequence for changing a required column. Include expand, backfill, deploy, contract, and rollback phases.

prompt_templates.title: Troubleshoot Failed Migration
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced incident response after partial migration failure.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this failed migration scenario. Identify likely causes, data integrity checks, safe recovery steps, and follow-up prevention tasks.

output_examples.input: Add an index to a large production table.
output_examples.output: The response should identify locking risk, recommend an online or concurrent index strategy, include rollback guidance, and list verification checks.

output_examples.input: Make an optional customer field required.
output_examples.output: The response should split the work into expand, backfill, validation, application deploy, and contract phases.

output_examples.input: Review a migration that drops a column.
output_examples.output: The response should warn about destructive changes, require usage checks, suggest a phased removal, and define rollback limits.

best_practices: Review migrations with the target database engine and framework in mind.
Test both forward migration and rollback paths before production deployment.
Separate schema changes from large data backfills when possible.
anti_patterns: Do not combine destructive schema changes with application changes in one release.
Do not assume rollback is safe after data has been transformed or deleted.
Do not run heavy backfills inside blocking schema migrations.

faq.question: Does this skill run migrations for me?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance for planning, writing, reviewing, and troubleshooting migration work.

faq.question: Which databases does it support?
faq.answer: The skill is general guidance. You should adapt recommendations to your database and migration framework.

faq.question: Can it guarantee zero-downtime deployment?
faq.answer: No. It helps plan safer steps, but production safety still requires testing and operational review.

faq.question: Can it review rollback safety?
faq.answer: Yes. It is designed to consider down methods, reversibility, and limits on rollback after data changes.

faq.question: Why is there a medium security warning?
faq.answer: The skill references a file outside its own folder using a parent-directory path. That dependency needs marketplace review.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for production database work?
faq.answer: It can support production planning, but final changes should be reviewed by engineers responsible for the system.
